verses:
anūpama mādhurī joḍī
hamāre śyāma⋅śyāmā kī
rasīlī rasabharī aṅkhiyā̐
hamāre śyāma⋅śyāmā kī

chavīlī hai adā bā̐kī sughara
sūrata madhura batiyā̐
laṭaka gardana kī mana basiyā
hamāre śyāma⋅śyāmā kī
mukuṭa aura candrikā māthe
adhara para pāna kī lālī
aho kaisī bhalī chavi hai
hamāre śyāma⋅śyāmā kī

paraspara mila ke jaba vihare̐
śrī vṛndāvana kī kuñjana me̐
nahī̐ baranata vane śobhā
hamāre śyāma⋅śyāmā kī

nahī̐ kucha lālasā dhana kī
nahī̐ nirvāṇa kī icchā
sakhī śyāmā ko de darśana
dayā ho śyāma⋅śyāmā kī
en_translation:
The unparalleled sweetness of the pair, of our Śyāma and Śyāmā! Their eyes are full of divine nectar and delight.

Their charming and graceful demeanor is beautiful, and Their words are sweet. The sway of Their necks captivates the mind. With crowns and moon-crests upon Their foreheads, and the redness of betel nuts upon Their lips—oh, how beautiful is this sight of our Śyāma and Śyāmā!

When They roam together in the groves of Śrī Vṛndāvana, Their beauty cannot be fully described.

I have no desire for wealth, nor do I wish for liberation. O friend! Just grant us the sight of Śyāmā; may we receive the mercy of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa.
